sources.notes: DHODH is the sole candidate for Miller syndrome — the filtering pipeline converged to a single gene under the recessive model. DNAH5 is included because: (1) the paper explicitly reports that both siblings in kindred 1 carry novel DNAH5 mutations, (2) DNAH5 variants explain the PCD/bronchiectasis phenotype in those siblings, (3) this is a confirmed positive finding reported with variant data, not merely a background citation. DNAH5 was initially flagged as a second candidate under recessive model, excluded as Miller syndrome cause, but confirmed as a comorbid finding. PMC full text strips gene symbols in XML template; DNAH5 identified from contextual description ('encodes a dynein heavy chain found in cilia, mutations are a well-known cause of primary ciliary dyskinesia'). Nager syndrome DHODH resequencing (negative) excluded. UMP synthase (UMPS) mentioned in pathway context only — excluded.
